name: Capablanca Chess
author: José Raúl Capablanca (c. 1920) / AGP port
description: Capablanca Chess (José Raúl Capablanca, c. 1920), on a 10x8 board. Adds two compound pieces: the Archbishop (a.k.a. Cardinal, bishop + knight) and the Chancellor (a.k.a. Marshall / Empress, rook + knight). The back rank is R N A B Q K B C N R, with pawns on the 2nd / 7th ranks. Pawns move and capture as in standard chess (double-step from start, en passant) and promote to Q/R/B/N/A/C on the last rank. The king castles by moving three squares toward a rook. Checkmate wins; stalemate and the usual fifty-move / repetition / insufficient-material situations draw.
